title: potentilla × matraensis
intro: Potentilla × fruticosa (previously referred to as Potentilla × matraensis), also known as shrubby cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species in the rose family, derived from the cross between Potentilla fruticosa and Potentilla rupestris. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Potentilla × fruticosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in the production of herbal supplements and medicinal creams.
